title: artemisia obtusiloba var. glabra
intro: Artemisia obtusiloba var. glabra, also known as glabrous wormwood, is a plant species in the daisy family and a variety of Artemisia obtusiloba, characterized by its glabrous leaves and stems. It is traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and treating digestive issues, due to its bioactive compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. In herbal preparations, Artemisia obtusiloba var. glabra is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in combination with other herbs for its synergistic effects in treating various health conditions.
